occurrenceRemarks: SOS Ref No. CA930A-908; Shrub; 1-2.5 ft. tall; yellow flowers. Frequent in flatter sandy bajada. 1000+ plants.
habitat: Bajada and foothills. Bajada slope 5-30 degrees, generally facing S72E. Holocene alluvium in wash and on bajada, below hills of: Pliocene and Pleistocene andesitic volcanic flows, sills, and plugs; Plio-Pleistocene Coso Formation (including undifferentiated sedimentary rock and rhyolitic pyroclastic rock); Plio-Pleistocene red arkosic sandstone; Mesozoic granite; Mesozoic intrusive rocks. Wash with sand soil, 2.5Y 4/4 olive brown. Slope with clay, sand, and gravel, 5YR 4/4 reddish brown. Open Joshua tree woodland and desert scrub.
associatedTaxa: Yucca brevifolia, Xylorhiza tortifolia, Ambrosia salsola, A. dumosa, Larrea tridentata, Lepidium fremontii, Grayia spinosa, Lycium andersonii, L. cooperi, Scutellaria mexicana, Krascheninnikovia lanata, Ericameria cooperi, Castilleja chromosa, Eriogonum fasciculatum, Atriplex confertifolia, Artemisia spinescens, ants, Ephedra nevadensis, Mirabilis laevis, Psorothamnus arborescens, Astragalus cf. mohavensis, Tetradymia, Vanessa cardui, Stipa hymenoides, Stephanomeria pauciflora, Elymus elymoides, Stipa speciosa, Delphinium cf. parishii, Othoptera: Acridomorpha, many annuals.
locality: Little Cactus Flats, west side. 8.8 road miles southeast on Cactus Flats Road SE 750 and SE 756 from U.S. 395, then 0.6 road miles west-northwest on SE 762 to foot of hills.
